**ISO 3166** is a standard published by the [International Organization for Standardization](/wiki/International_Organization_for_Standardization "International Organization for Standardization") (ISO) that defines [codes](/wiki/Code "Code") for the names of [countries](/wiki/Country "Country"), [dependent territories](/wiki/Dependent_territory "Dependent territory"), special areas of geographical interest, and their principal [subdivisions](/wiki/Administrative_divisions "Administrative divisions") (e.g., [provinces](/wiki/Province "Province") or [states](/wiki/State_(administrative_division) "State (administrative division)")). The official name of the standard is ***Codes for the representation of names of countries and their subdivisions***.

## Parts

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=ISO_3166&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Parts")]

It consists of three parts:[[1]](#cite_note-iso-info-1)

* **[ISO 3166-1](/wiki/ISO_3166-1 "ISO 3166-1")**, *Codes for the representation of names of countries and their subdivisions – Part 1: Country code*, defines codes for the names of countries, dependent territories, and special areas of geographical interest. It defines three sets of [country codes](/wiki/Country_code "Country code"):
  + [ISO 3166-1 alpha-2](/wiki/ISO_3166-1_alpha-2 "ISO 3166-1 alpha-2") – two-letter country codes which are the most widely used of the three, and used most prominently for the [Internet](/wiki/Internet "Internet")'s [country code top-level domains](/wiki/Country_code_top-level_domain "Country code top-level domain") (with a few exceptions).
  + [ISO 3166-1 alpha-3](/wiki/ISO_3166-1_alpha-3 "ISO 3166-1 alpha-3") – three-letter country codes which allow a better visual association between the codes and the country names than the alpha-2 codes.
  + [ISO 3166-1 numeric](/wiki/ISO_3166-1_numeric "ISO 3166-1 numeric") – three-digit country codes which are identical to those developed and maintained by the [United Nations Statistics Division](/wiki/United_Nations_Statistics_Division "United Nations Statistics Division"), with the advantage of script ([writing system](/wiki/Writing_system "Writing system")) independence, and hence useful for people or systems using non-[Latin scripts](/wiki/Latin_script "Latin script").
* **[ISO 3166-2](/wiki/ISO_3166-2 "ISO 3166-2")**, *Codes for the representation of names of countries and their subdivisions – Part 2: Country subdivision code*, defines codes for the names of the principal subdivisions (e.g., provinces, states, departments, regions) of all countries coded in ISO 3166-1.
* **[ISO 3166-3](/wiki/ISO_3166-3 "ISO 3166-3")**, *Codes for the representation of names of countries and their subdivisions – Part 3: Code for formerly used names of countries*, defines codes for country names which have been deleted from ISO 3166-1 since its first publication in 1974.

## Editions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=ISO_3166&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Editions")]

The first edition of ISO 3166, which included only alphabetic country codes, was published in 1974. The second edition, published in 1981, also included numeric country codes, with the third and fourth editions published in 1988 and 1993 respectively. The fifth edition, published between 1997 and 1999, was expanded into three parts to include codes for subdivisions and former countries.[[1]](#cite_note-iso-info-1)

## ISO 3166 Maintenance Agency

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=ISO_3166&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: ISO 3166 Maintenance Agency")]

The ISO 3166 standard is maintained by the **ISO 3166 Maintenance Agency** (**ISO 3166/MA**), located at the ISO central office in [Geneva](/wiki/Geneva "Geneva"). Originally it was located at the [Deutsches Institut für Normung](/wiki/Deutsches_Institut_f%C3%BCr_Normung "Deutsches Institut für Normung") (DIN) in [Berlin](/wiki/Berlin "Berlin"). Its principal tasks are:[[1]](#cite_note-iso-info-1)

* To add and to eliminate country names and to assign code elements to them;
* To publish lists of country names and code elements;
* To maintain a reference list of all country code elements and subdivision code elements used and their period of use;
* To issue newsletters announcing changes to the code tables;
* To advise users on the application of ISO 3166.

### Members

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=ISO_3166&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Members")]

There are fifteen experts with voting rights on the ISO 3166/MA.[[1]](#cite_note-iso-info-1) Nine are representatives of national [standards organizations](/wiki/Standards_organization "Standards organization"):

* [Association française de normalisation](/wiki/Association_fran%C3%A7aise_de_normalisation "Association française de normalisation") (AFNOR) – France
* [American National Standards Institute](/wiki/American_National_Standards_Institute "American National Standards Institute") (ANSI) – United States
* [British Standards Institution](/wiki/British_Standards_Institution "British Standards Institution") (BSI) – United Kingdom
* [Deutsches Institut für Normung](/wiki/Deutsches_Institut_f%C3%BCr_Normung "Deutsches Institut für Normung") (DIN) – Germany
* [Japanese Industrial Standards Committee](/wiki/Japanese_Industrial_Standards_Committee "Japanese Industrial Standards Committee") (JISC) – Japan
* [Standards Australia](/wiki/Standards_Australia "Standards Australia") (SA) – Australia
* [Kenya Bureau of Standards](/wiki/Kenya_Bureau_of_Standards "Kenya Bureau of Standards") (KEBS) – Kenya
* [Standardization Administration of China](/wiki/Standardization_Administration_of_China "Standardization Administration of China") (SAC) – China
* [Swedish Standards Institute](/wiki/Swedish_Standards_Institute "Swedish Standards Institute") (SIS) – Sweden

The other six are representatives of major [United Nations agencies](/wiki/List_of_specialized_agencies_of_the_United_Nations "List of specialized agencies of the United Nations") or other [international organizations](/wiki/International_organization "International organization") who are all users of ISO 3166-1:

* [International Atomic Energy Agency](/wiki/International_Atomic_Energy_Agency "International Atomic Energy Agency") (IAEA)
* [International Civil Aviation Organization](/wiki/International_Civil_Aviation_Organization "International Civil Aviation Organization") (ICAO)
* [International Telecommunication Union](/wiki/International_Telecommunication_Union "International Telecommunication Union") (ITU)
* [Internet Corporation for Assigned Names and Numbers](/wiki/Internet_Corporation_for_Assigned_Names_and_Numbers "Internet Corporation for Assigned Names and Numbers") (ICANN)
* [Universal Postal Union](/wiki/Universal_Postal_Union "Universal Postal Union") (UPU)
* [United Nations Economic Commission for Europe](/wiki/United_Nations_Economic_Commission_for_Europe "United Nations Economic Commission for Europe") (UNECE)

The ISO 3166/MA has further associated members who do not participate in the votes but who, through their expertise, have significant influence on the decision-taking procedure in the maintenance agency.

## Codes beginning with "X"

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=ISO_3166&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Codes beginning with \"X\"")]

Country codes beginning with "X" are used for private custom use (reserved), never for official codes. Despite the words "private custom", the use may include other public standards. ISO affirms that no country code beginning with X will ever be standardised.[[a]](#cite_note-2) Examples of X codes include:

* The ISO 3166-based [NATO](/wiki/NATO "NATO") [country codes](/wiki/List_of_NATO_country_codes "List of NATO country codes") ([STANAG 1059](/wiki/STANAG_1059 "STANAG 1059"), 9th edition) use "X" codes for imaginary exercise countries ranging from XXB for "Brownland" to XXY for "Yellowland", as well as for major commands such as XXE for [SHAPE](/wiki/Supreme_Headquarters_Allied_Powers_Europe "Supreme Headquarters Allied Powers Europe") or XXS for [SACLANT](/wiki/Supreme_Allied_Commander_Atlantic "Supreme Allied Commander Atlantic").
* [X currencies defined in ISO 4217](/wiki/ISO_4217#X_currencies "ISO 4217").
